title: read.collection/read.mutations cursor + limit hardening (op-store pagination under a ~1M-row log)
commits: 441d071 docs(0.8.0): Slice 33 design memo — op-store read.* cursor+limit hardening (step-13 (collection_name,id) index; EXPLAIN before/after)
a2b8e19 test(0.8.0): Slice 33 RED — EXPLAIN index gate + bounded large-log pagination + clamp/cursor edges; step-13 migration/DDL tests
fcd4ea9 feat(0.8.0): Slice 33 GREEN schema — step-13 additive index operational_mutations(collection_name,id); SCHEMA_VERSION 12->13
6dc267c fix(0.8.0): Slice 33 GREEN engine — read_collection cursor hardening (negative after_id clamped to start); SELECT rides the step-13 index
c8fd209 docs(0.8.0): Slice 33 — op-store index-driven pagination contract (step-13); migrations index-only-no-marker note; DOC-INDEX rows
2bbf7a3 test(0.8.0): Slice 33 — advance schema-head pins 12->13 (step sequence, open user_version, tokenizer phase-A/B v10 prefix slice by absolute length)
findings_addressed: G3 / F4-READ — op-store paginated read-back (read.collection / read.mutations) is now bounded + index-driven under a genuine large multi-collection operational_mutations log; clamp + after_id cursor robust at the edges (limit==0, over-MAX clamp, negative/past-end after_id, unknown collection). No new AC id (acceptance.md locked at 0.6.0/AC-073); bound by gap label + TDD test names.
schema_change: step-13 additive index operational_mutations(collection_name,id) (operational_mutations_collection_id_idx); SCHEMA_VERSION 12->13. Forward-only, no table reshape, no column add, no DROP. Pure CREATE INDEX so the accretion guard (CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN only) does not flag it and no exemption marker is required. Companion doc-mirror src/rust/crates/fathomdb-schema/migrations/013_op_store_collection_index.sql added (matches the steps 5/8/9/11 .sql-mirror pattern).

explain_before_after.query: SELECT id, collection_name, record_key, op_kind, payload_json, schema_id, write_cursor FROM operational_mutations WHERE collection_name = ?1 AND id > ?2 ORDER BY id LIMIT ?3
explain_before_after.before: SEARCH operational_mutations USING INTEGER PRIMARY KEY (rowid>?) -- rides the id PK, filters collection_name row-by-row over the id-ordered log; O(rows-scanned) for a small collection inside a large multi-collection log
explain_before_after.after: SEARCH operational_mutations USING INDEX operational_mutations_collection_id_idx (collection_name=? AND id>?) -- index-driven: no SCAN, no USE TEMP B-TREE FOR ORDER BY, not the id-PK walk; the leading collection_name equality fixes the prefix and the trailing id serves both the after_id range and ORDER BY id; O(page)
explain_before_after.measured_via: EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on a migrated DB seeded with a multi-collection log (small = every 100th row inside a larger bulk log); pinned in pr_g3_read_collection.rs::read_collection_plan_is_index_driven_no_scan_no_temp_btree

byte_freeze_verified: true — git diff --exit-code (both vs baseline e1827c4 and vs rebase target main f91542c) on src/python/tests/test_no_recovery_surface.py, src/ts/tests/no-recovery-surface.test.ts, src/rust/crates/fathomdb/tests/no_recovery_surface.rs: byte-unchanged. cargo test -p fathomdb --test no_recovery_surface: green.
search_pin_green: true — Slice 10 Search byte-identity pin pr_g10_filtered_knn.rs::unfiltered_phase1_sql_byte_identical_to_0_7_2: green. No search-path / read_search_in_tx change.
regression_pins_green: Slice 20 pr_g8_dangling_edges (8 pass), Slice 30 pr_g2_get_by_id (7 pass) + pr_g3_read_collection (12 pass incl. new), schema migrations (12 pass incl. step-13), compatibility/pr_g0_identity/pr_g1_tokenizer_* head-version pins (advanced 12->13). Py functional-retrieve (5 pass) + pyright 0/0; TS functional-retrieve (67 pass).
blockers_encountered: None blocking. (1) agent-verify AC-037 netns-deny-egress: BLOCKER (rootless userns+netns unavailable in this env) — the allowed environmental exemption, CI-covered at Slice 40. (2) agent-verify AC-050c removal-detect: FAIL on src/python/tests/test_surface.py (two renamed test functions not listed in CHANGELOG Removed) — PRE-EXISTING on the canonical baseline e1827c4 (reproduces identically on the untouched canonical repo); NOT introduced by Slice 33 (zero diff to test_surface.py). Surfaced for the orchestrator; not a Slice 33 regression.
additional_changes_made_in_scope: Mechanical schema-head pin advances (consequence of SCHEMA_VERSION 12->13, AGENTS.md §1 version-bump exception): compatibility.rs step sequence +13; pr_g0_identity.rs head user_version asserts SCHEMA_VERSION (was literal 12); pr_g1_tokenizer_crash_recovery.rs head literals 12->13; pr_g1_tokenizer_recall.rs phase-B head 12->13 AND V10_MIGRATIONS now slices by absolute prefix length 10 (was MIGRATIONS.len()-2, brittle — would have shifted to v11 once a step was added).

read_collection_in_tx cursor normalization: after = after_id.unwrap_or(0).max(0) — a negative after_id is explicitly clamped to the start of the log (ids are >=1). Semantically identical to before for the happy path; removes the negative-cursor ambiguity. SELECT shape unchanged (still rides the new index). No signature change.
Reverted an unrelated npm-install package-lock.json version bump (0.6.1->0.7.2) so the slice diff stays scoped.
agent_verify_result: lint/typecheck/test green; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean; pyright -p src/python 0/0; mkdocs build clean (via .venv/bin/mkdocs). agent-security: AC-037 netns BLOCKER (allowed/environmental) + AC-050c removal-detect FAIL (PRE-EXISTING on baseline, not this slice).
next_step_for_orchestrator: Slice 33 merged --no-ff onto local main (rebased onto Slice 27's f91542c; both DOC-INDEX/STATUS rows kept). Run the codex §9 review of the Slice 33 diff next. Two non-Slice-33 items to note: (a) AC-037 netns is the standing env exemption (Slice 40 CI); (b) AC-050c removal-detect FAILs on the BASELINE for src/python/tests/test_surface.py — a pre-existing CHANGELOG Removed-section gap independent of Slice 33; consider a separate cleanup. No push/origin touched.
